items.headline: Equinix data center permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center building receives permit at 131 Great Oaks Bl.

items.headline: Chick-fil-A renovation permitted on Blossom Hill Road
items.summary: An $809,557 demolition and addition project is cleared to build at 1162 Blossom Hill Rd, indicating the chain is expanding or rebuilding its existing San José location.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument art installation agreement approved
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with the Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for a Seeds of Peace Monument artwork by Mario Chiodo, to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The artwork memorializes peace and will be installed in a public city park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District FY 2026-27 budget set
items.summary: City Council took action on the FY 2026-27 budget report and annual assessment for the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District, which funds improvements and services in that commercial area.

items.headline: City Council updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed an update to the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan covering 2015–2040. The agenda does not provide specifics on what changes were proposed or adopted.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution to appoint a permanent City Manager. This fills the position on a permanent basis after what may have been a period of interim leadership.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates urban water master plan amid drought risk
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The plan addresses how the city will manage water supply during shortages, a live issue for Bay Area water reliability.

items.headline: City opens 2026 spring grants for community events and neighborhood programs
items.summary: The City Council considered applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program spring cycle on May 18. Residents and nonprofits can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood initiatives.
